entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 20 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-20 04:14AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Australia news LIVE: CMO urges masks indoors as COVID cases grow across the nation; Plibersek flags legislative change after State of Environment report
  • Australia’s new world champion: Eleanor Patterson wins high jump gold
  • Natalie Portman’s buff superhero hammers the beauty myth
  • Resort fever: Lindeman Island put up for sale
  • Drummoyne MP John Sidoti found corrupt by ICAC
  • ‘True Liberals’ called to action to repair decimated WA party
  • Outcry as planning minister reopens abalone farm proposal in south-west Victoria
  • No further sanctions for swimmer sent home ahead of Commonwealth Games
  • Roberts-Smith told ‘deliberate lie’ to conceal killing, court hears
  • Mother assisting police after three children found dead in WA house fire
  • Iran endorses Putin’s war, US says Russia ‘preparing to annex territory’
  • Albanese wants MPs to wear masks in Parliament next week but won’t support mandates
  • Ambassador Caroline Kennedy to arrive in Australia at ‘critical time’ for the region
  • Reserve Bank pandemic policy contributed to inflation: Philip Lowe
  • ACT to use rego fees to reward EV drivers and replace lost revenue
  • Reserve Bank culture, board and inflation target to be review focus
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • Best of cartoons, July 20, 2022
  • Best of cartoons, July 19, 2022
  • Chief doctor pleads for mask-wearing amid ‘significant’ new threat
  • Plibersek promises new laws next year to tackle rapid environmental decline
  • After a decade in the wilderness, Labor still lost on the environment
  • Cashed up and employed: RBA says households can withstand higher rates
  • Six men arrested over alleged importation of $50 million of cocaine
  • Second man charged after girl shot in Sydney’s south
  • Mother of alleged Alameddine clan member charged over ‘fake’ COVID-19 vaccination certificate
  • ‘Act quick’: Government urged to vaccinate at-risk groups against monkeypox
  • Stable population is key to saving our declining wildlife
  • From the Archives, 1972: These girls prefer travelling to marriage
  • In the Herald: July 20, 1980
  • Turning the dial and other great moments
  • Alleged terror group planned attacks on Sydney naval base, court and Mardi Gras, court told
  • New rules would stop Liberals ‘carving up seats like in Lawrence of Arabia’
  • Coastal erosion warning as NSW prepares for strong winds and swell
  • Queenwood principal to head 640-year-old British boys’ school
  • If this is the new normal, Britain is not ready for Australian-style summers
  • ‘Don’t do it Emmanuel’: Interrupting emu earns global fame
  • Village fires, warped roads and the Queen kept working: How the UK suffered through its hottest day
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Destruction
  • Schools insist students carry clear backpacks after Uvalde massacre
  • Australia asked to help in Ukraine war crimes investigation
  • Candidate who flirted with abandoning net zero knocked out of Tory race to be PM
  • Hastie, Birmingham look to UK for ways to recruit women, diverse candidates without quotas
  • Doctored tweet of Australian ‘war crime’ part of Beijing ‘sentiment mobilisation’
  • A ‘just war’: West has a moral obligation to help defeat Russia
  • Australian mine workers arrested in Indonesia after drugs discovery
  • Electoral authority slams Bolsonaro’s claims of fraudulent election to come
  • IBAC report: Guy claims Labor not fit to govern, but PM says problems fixed
  • COVID-denying fake doctor who handed out 1200 exemptions fined $25,000
  • Joe is one of many New Zealanders forced to sleep on the streets of Sydney
  • ‘We are going broke’: Truckie fuel protest clogs West Gate Bridge
  • One man’s change of heart has put a historic deal in jeopardy
  • BHP joins Indian steel giant to tackle industry’s carbon problem
  • ASX heading for best day in weeks as tech stocks soar
  • Stranger Things helps Netflix slow the bleeding
  • Netflix buys Australian animation studio behind Happy Feet, The Lego Movie
  • The livestream shopping craze is coming to Australia
  • Horror year of wild weather sparks jump in insurance complaints
  • Bill Papas business partner denies hiding hundreds of thousands of dollars from court
  • ANZ and Suncorp merger ‘important test’ for ACCC, says former competition tsar
  • ‘Perfect match’: Gladys Berejiklian on life after politics and her new job at Optus
  • ‘Extremely disappointed’: Mirvac’s AMP office fund raid draws fire
  • What JB Hi-Fi’s result says about Australian consumers’ buying frenzy
  • The Age and Sydney Morning Herald launch new podcast showcasing culture journalism
  • Australia could unleash a cultural renaissance by paying artists a living wage
  • Sarah Ferguson wastes no time putting her own stamp on ABC’s 7.30
  • Unexpected twist along Elton John’s road to retirement
  • Meshel Laurie slams The Project for ‘token’ use of Indigenous guests
  • ‘Europe is burning’: How a heatwave engulfed a continent
  • Environmental destruction is a threat to Australia’s imagination
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • China has a hand in Sri Lanka’s economic calamity
  • Japan plans state funeral Sept. 27 for slain former leader Shinzo Abe
  • Russia seeks to annex Ukraine’s east, south later this year, U.S. says
  • Ukraine live briefing: White House warns of Moscow annexation plans; Russia and Ukraine undertake more diplomatic outreach
  • Michael Krepon, campaigner to limit nuclear arms, dies at 75
  • Keep cool and carry on? Britons struggle through hottest day on record.
  • Russia’s Gazprom sends force majeure letter to European clients
  • Putin makes rare international trip to Iran amid isolation from the West
  • Bidens welcome Ukrainian first lady to the White House
  • U.K. sees hottest day on record, with temperatures hitting 40 Celsius
  • Europe sizzles in record heat wave as thousands flee wildfires
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Who will be the next U.K. prime minister? 3 candidates are still standing.
  • China says it will take ‘forceful measures’ if Pelosi visits Taiwan
  • Australia leads continents in mammal extinctions, climate report says
  • U.S. requests more information on UAE arrest of Khashoggi lawyer
  • Ukraine’s farmers become the latest target of Russian missiles
  • 'Forgotten' US victims of Mexican drug lord want justice
  • Amnesty Intl says Myanmar army has laid landmines in Kayah
  • 3 migrants dead, 12 injured in Mexico crash
  • UN experts: Terrorist threat remains high in conflict areas
  • Russia pounds Ukraine as Putin gets Iran's backing
  • Putin, in Tehran, gets strong support from Iran over Ukraine
  • Patrick Michaels, outspoken climate change contrarian, dies at 72
  • Police may have cracked a 1975 killing — by digging through trash
  • Brett Kavanaugh’s neighbors: For abortion rights but tired of protests
  • Jill Biden tests the power of niceties
  • D.A. asks court to dismiss New York bodega worker’s murder charge
  • After Uvalde massacre, Dallas district plans to require clear backpacks
  • Secret Service cannot recover texts; no new details for Jan. 6 committee
  • Trans Chick-fil-A worker told to be ‘honored’ by catcalls, suit says
  • Uvalde intensifies doubts over whether tiny police agencies make sense
  • Sesame Place apologizes after Muppet seems to snub Black girls in video
  • A camper scared off a bear — then the grizzly came back and killed her
  • Bystander rescues 4 kids from fire, then jumps out window to save a 5th
  • Indiana AG’s comments endangered abortion provider, complaint says
  • An attack left a woman in a coma. She woke up and accused her brother.
  • Post Politics Now: House passes measure that would codify same-sex marriage into law with bipartisan support
  • GOP fake electors ‘targets’ in Georgia election fraud inquiry
  • U.S. says Bannon ‘chose to show contempt’
  • Prosecutor: Steve Bannon thumbed his nose at the law
  • Opening statements soon in Bannon trial after judge rejects delay
  • Netflix loses nearly 1 million subscribers, and its stock soars
  • Gas prices have plunged 10 percent since their June peak
  • Toys R Us tries a comeback, again
  • What to know as Elon Musk’s rocky deal with Twitter heads to trial
  • Judge grants Elon Musk an October court date, in early win for Twitter
  • U.K. won’t ban video game loot boxes following 2-year inquiry
  • U.S. touts progress in hypersonic arms race with China, Russia
  • Townhouses with expandable floor plan in Bowie, Md.
  • Good flashings defend against roof leaks
  • Mixed-use development coming to Howard University
  • Rajeev Misra Must Be Doing Something Right
  • The Dollar Bulls Are Looking Increasingly Like a Cult
  • Netflix Shouldn’t Take a Victory Lap Just Yet
  • Friendship Is the Best Way to Counter China in the Pacific
  • Why Marburg Virus Is an Increasing Threat in Africa
  • How Thailand Is Blazing Trails on Gay Rights, Legalizing Marijuana
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Chip Neutrality Scuppered Nvidia Deal to Buy Arm: QuickTake
  • In a nasty era, insisting on basic politeness is a revolutionary idea
  • How bad is BA.5? Dr. Leana Wen answers your questions.
  • War resumes in Ukraine and so does Russian atrocity
  • There are too many wild cards to forecast the Ukraine war
  • Trump should hope for a crowded 2024 GOP field. Really.
  • The end of affirmative action at colleges could be a good thing
  • Want to help fight climate change? Don’t lift tariffs on Chinese imports.
  • Save the Biden agenda from Joe Manchin
  • Democrats have to decide: Are they about change or the status quo?
  • Is Trump finally becoming a serious candidate? You’ve got to be kidding.
  • Save local news. Our democracy depends on it.
  • Why the Tory leadership race matters
  • Manchin wasn’t the only one to kill climate action
  • Maryland GOP helps Trump — but likely hurts itself — with gubernatorial pick
  • New discoveries in Emmett Till’s murder reinforce the need for truth
  • Don’t stop at the Chips Act, Congress
  • Whether or not Biden runs, he must not bank on Trump
  • Shocker: Almost no one trusts TV news
  • Democrats are done ‘Waiting for Manchin.’ Time to get on with it.
  • I’m a Texas gun owner. The Texas way of guns is an American failure.
  • I survived Epstein and Maxwell’s sex ring. Then the gaslighting began.
  • A burning desire
  • 377 good guys with a gun
  • Biden just declared a national emergency. We officially have a hostage crisis.
  • Secret Service texts should have been preserved
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 20 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-20 04:02AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: what is ‘hybrid immunity’ and would Hong Kong benefit as several health experts say?
  • Almost all 8 perfect scorers in Hong Kong’s university entrance exams from elite schools
  • China’s new data export rules cast doubt over Hong Kong’s role as gateway to the mainland
  • Covid and closed borders: Hong Kong has avoided inflation. But how long can this last?
  • Bringing women back into the workplace is essential for Hong Kong’s pandemic recovery
  • China’s buyers are snapping up abodes in Marco Rubio’s Florida home base, splurging US$6.1 billion on US real estate to top US list for 10th year
  • Senior Hong Kong police officer ‘faces suspension for alleged attack on 2 colleagues after consuming alcohol at dinner’
  • Hong Kong publishers adopt cautious attitude ahead of Asia’s largest annual book fair
  • HK$14 million theft: Hong Kong helper’s sentence increased by 17 months
  • Kung fu star Bruce Lee’s last Hong Kong home to be recreated as a digital shrine for fans
  • Jobless rate in Hong Kong falls to 4.7 per cent as Covid situation improves, social-distancing measures eased
  • Inter Milan boss and Suning founder’s son Steven Zhang liable for US$255 million in landmark verdict against China’s keepwell undertakings
  • 8 Hong Kong students bag perfect scores in university entrance exams, but number of those hitting minimum qualifying mark drops
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong care home staff ‘to undergo weekly PCR tests’ in government’s first move to shore up defences; 3,256 new cases logged
  • 4 Hong Kong jobseekers lose HK$1.25 million in online shopping scam known as ‘boosting sales’
  • ‘Anti-China clampdown’ remarks by top state official on Hong Kong affairs not targeting difference in views, Beijing heavyweight says
  • Second man arrested in Hong Kong over case of 23 unauthorised alerts from TVB app
  • China-India border: Beijing’s new highway plans near disputed territory expected to spark concern in Delhi
  • China’s approvals for new coal plants rebound amid renewed focus on energy security after last year’s power crisis: Greenpeace
  • Chinese students vow to fight penalty for distributing rainbow flags at Tsinghua University
  • Senate ‘test vote’ shows support for bill providing $52 billion to US semiconductor industry to out-compete China
  • More than just tuna: US casts a wider net to compete with China in the Pacific
  • China on cusp of joining high-income club, but slowdown raises spectre of middle-income trap
  • US adds China to list of countries where Americans face elevated risk of wrongful detention
  • China travellers reconsider flights as high oil prices see fuel surcharge surge tenfold
  • ‘Surprising shortfalls’ in China military logistics suggest lack of conflict readiness: US analysts
  • EU and China hold trade talks after delays, though few advances are made
  • Global heatwaves show urgency of adapting to changing climate
  • China cut US debt holding amid ‘risk of possible conflict’
  • Tech war: broader ban on ASML sales to China will hurt firm’s revenue and set back Beijing’s domestic chip-making drive, analysts say
  • China’s Fujian aircraft carrier doesn’t have radar and weapon systems yet, photos show
  • Chinese scientists say penguins stopped flying and became great swimmers long before polar ice sheets formed
  • What US move to defund Chinese labs might mean for science and future pandemics
  • China denies inviting European leaders to meet Xi, but sources confirm approach has been made
  • Executives at Fosun-owned online dating giant Baihe Jiayuan arrested for embezzlement after report of ‘missing’ managers
  • China praises Ukraine mediator Macron as Beijing hopes to renew EU ties
  • Former US defence chief says one-China policy has ‘outlived its usefulness’
  • Why China is likely to miss Shinzo Abe and his vision as battles loom on many fronts
  • Covid-19 in China: cases near 700 as Shanghai rolls out testing blitz
  • Chinese boy goes viral after he storms out of planetarium documentary over Long March rockets factual errors
  • Hardline Indonesian cleric Habib Razieq Shihab released from prison
  • ‘Failure to prosecute’ Japan turtle killers may embolden culprits, activists say
  • Myanmar junta troops lay landmines around churches, homes and toilets, Amnesty International says
  • Vietnam, Cambodia and Brunei join Malaysia on US trafficking blacklist
  • How the West’s sanctions on Russia are fuelling political and economic instability – and resentment – in South Asia
  • Malaysia won’t budge ‘an inch’ over Sabah as Philippine heirs chase US$15 billion claim
  • South Korea’s cheaper answer to F-35 jet makes first test flight
  • China’s miHoYo, creator of hit game Genshin Impact, picks Singapore as base of metaverse unit, plans ‘hundreds of jobs’
  • Thai cave rescue remembered in new Ron Howard film ‘Thirteen Lives’
  • Singapore distances itself from troubled crypto firms as it plans tighter regulations
  • Thailand introduces new ‘non-stinky’ variety of polarising durian fruit
  • Malaysia won’t go bankrupt like Sri Lanka, finance minister tells MPs – even as its China palm oil exports tumble
  • Shinzo Abe’s death ‘could have been avoided’ if his security acted faster, new analysis shows
  • Australia’s ‘utter failure’ on conservation, environment exposed in ‘disturbing’ government report
  • Ukraine war: Vladimir Putin warns EU that gas supplies could keep dwindling
  • Ex-Trump adviser Steve Bannon felt he was ‘above the law’, prosecutor says as contempt trial begins
  • Ukraine first lady Olena Zelenska meets Jill Biden at White House during high-profile US trip
  • Why US should encourage China to lead Afghanistan’s long-term reconstruction
  • Twitter gets a win over Elon Musk with trial fast-tracked for October
  • Vladimir Putin visits Iran on first trip outside former USSR since Ukraine war began
  • Ukraine war: Zelensky sacks more intelligence service officials after numerous defections to the Russian side
  • Methanol found in blood of South African teenagers who died in bar in June
  • Temperature hits 40 degrees in Britain for first time on record
  • Coronavirus: ‘virus is not done with us yet’ warns expert as new Covid-19 variants are better at evading antibodies
  • ‘Peace on our terms’: Russia keeps up bombardment of cities across Ukraine
  • UK’s Boris Johnson takes back seat, enjoys the ride during final weeks in power
  • Donald Trump eyes early launch to 2024 White House run, casting shadow over November midterms
  • Alibaba, HSBC drive Hong Kong stocks to one-week high as risk appetite recovers, Fed concerns ease
  • SEC’s Gensler says it’s unclear if US and China will reach a deal to avoid stock delistings
  • More buyers of incomplete flats with stage payment schemes face higher risk of default as rising interest rates spur price falls
  • Douyin, China’s version of TikTok, calls a truce in war on long-form video with iQiyi licensing deal
  • Start-ups in NFTs, blockchain-based DeFi can rise from crypto-crash ashes to become unicorns, says KPMG and HSBC report
  • Shanghai bourse to appoint market makers for SMIC, other stocks on tech board in boon for Citic and rival brokerages
  • School district home rents drop by the most in 2 years as more Hong Kong residents head for the emigration queue
  • Henan bank crisis threatens to derail China’s efforts to maintain a stable yuan
  • Detective vs Sleuths movie review: Lau Ching-wan in wildly chaotic police thriller with urgent moral messages at its core
  • The big mistake of Philippines tourism promotion video? Assuming consumers of social media think deeply about anything
  • Singapore restaurants reimagine grandma’s home cooking and revive lost dishes from Peranakan, or Straits Chinese, cuisine
  • Diabetes emergency: a type 1 diabetic’s insulin overdose and his first-hand account of what he did to survive
  • Decision to Leave: Cannes Film Festival best director winner Park Chan-wook on the movie’s blend of romance and police drama
  • Dismay at closure of one of Hong Kong’s oldest traditional cafes, Mido Cafe, announced in an ambiguous note – but is it permanent?
  • World’s 50 Best Restaurants 2022: Only 1 Asian restaurant in the top 20 as European fine dining dominates again
  • Meet Louis Vuitton’s high jewellery designer who’s making priceless pieces that are ‘actually fun to wear’
  • Jam or cream first on scones? A top pastry chef’s verdict, a big lesson learned and his cravings for Hong Kong food in London
  • 4 times Hwang Min-hyun proved he’s K-pop’s ‘Prince Charming’: besides his royal-looking onstage attire with Wanna One and Nu’Est, the Netflix K-drama star is apparently charming too
  • The Kardashians’ obsession with having the same baby daddy: Khloé is having a second child with cheater Tristan Thompson – just like Kim, Kourtney, Kylie also stuck it out for the sake of their kids
  • 17 female celebrity besties both on and off screen, from Marvel’s Salma Hayek and Pirates of the Caribbean star Penélope Cruz, to Ariana Grande and Elizabeth Gillies, to Zendaya and Hunter Schafer
  • Meet Princess Raiyah of Jordan, married to Roald Dahl’s grandson: the half-sister of King Abdullah II once attended a school where Queen Elizabeth and mum Queen Noor are presidents
  • Meet the 6 women Elvis Presley fell ‘in love’ with: he married Priscilla, had a fling with Ann-Margret, and was with Ginger Alden till the day he died – but which of them later married Caitlyn Jenner?
  • All of Jennifer Lopez’s wedding dresses to date: she just wore a gown from her old film for her Vegas nuptials to Ben Affleck, but went for Valentino with Cris Judd, and Vera Wang to wed Marc Anthony
  • Fan Bingbing lost 6kg in 7 days from bigu fasting, but fans are upset: the X-Men actress documented her weight loss journey on Xiaohongshu but was slammed for setting a bad example
  • Which are the best countries offering citizenship by investment? 40 nations – from Thailand to the UAE – offer ‘golden visas’ to high net worth individuals splurging millions on property
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Judge grants Twitter fast-track trial to decide fate of $44bn Musk deal

  • Western democracies have a talent problem
  • Netflix loses 1mn subscribers but defections are fewer than feared

entry.content:

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • US adds Vietnam, Cambodia, Macao to trafficking blacklist
  • China to fine ride-hailing giant Didi more than US$1 billion: Reports
  • Striking workers at South Korea shipyard in talks to end siege
  • Thai central bank to ensure uninterrupted economic recovery
  • Patterson wins world high jump gold with personal best leap
  • Brazil's dos Santos wins 400m hurdles world final, Warholm denied
  • Ceh dethrones Sweden's Stahl to win world discus title
  • Putin says Ukraine did not make good on preliminary peace deal
  • Briton Wightman takes shock 1,500m gold with dad commentating
  • 3 migrants dead, 12 injured in Mexico crash

entry.title: Guardian: 20 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Erdoğan keeps Putin waiting in awkward moment ahead of Tehran talks
  • Sri Lanka holds its breath as parliament chooses new president amid crisis
  • US House passes bill to protect right to same-sex and interracial marriage

  • England no longer have anything to fear despite Spain’s fresh challenge | Jonathan Liew

  • Kemi Badenoch knocked out of Tory leadership race
  • Dill or no dill? Herb prices rocket in Australia but consumers told to bide their thyme

  • Italian police thwart illegal sale of Artemisia Gentileschi painting
  • Footage shows UPS driver stumbling from heat while delivering packages in Arizona
  • We’re going to need a bigger boat: the rise of the megashark
  • Britain is boiling – and the government wants to dramatically expand UK aviation | Leo Murray
  • Covid: European governments must urgently boost monitoring, WHO says
  • We have downsized to a smaller home and it has freed us to live the life we want | Damian Barr
  • ‘I put my trauma on display’ – Keala Settle on hating her signature song This Is Me
  • Bolsonaro’s attack on Brazil’s electoral system sparks outrage
  • Ukrainian boy held hostage by Russia tells of cleaning up torture rooms
  • XR protesters smash windows of News UK over coverage of Britain’s heatwave
  • Jackie Chan-produced action movie films in devastated Syrian city
  • Yes, Britain had a heatwave in 1976. No, it was nothing like the crisis we’re in now | Ella Gilbert
  • Should I feel bad for still wanting to have sex now I am 87?
  • Kosher phone dispute grips ultra-Orthodox Tel Aviv suburb
  • Operation Julie: famed LSD drug bust becomes a musical
  • ‘People would say, “This is hideous!’”: how ‘tacky’ Portuguese style took over the UK
  • David Squires on … Euro 2022 heating up and the cartoonist’s curse
  • My name is Brian: the prisoner whose treatment put Switzerland on trial
  • It might be a cliche but I am feeling the urge to steal | Zoe Williams
  • ‘I said, Don, it’s time for you to reveal’: 50 years later, the truth behind American Pie
  • North Korean labour could be sent to rebuild Donbas, Russian ambassador says
  • ‘A bigger paycheck? I’d rather watch the sunset!’: is this the end of ambition?
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
